example_prompt: Send money to multiple mobile money recipients in a single batch payout request.

input_schema.description: Request body is an array of payout objects. Each object describes one recipient.

customerId.description: Existing customer UUID. Takes precedence over customerObject.

response_schema.description: HTTP 202 — batch accepted. Processing is asynchronous. Bictorys fires a webhook for each individual payout when it completes.

id.description: Batch payout identifier for tracking the group.

gotchas: Bulk payout access requires a separate secret key obtained from Bictorys support — it is not available through the standard dashboard. Contact Bictorys to enable this capability and receive a sandbox test key before using this endpoint.

payment_type AND country_code are both required query parameters. Append them to the URL: POST /pay/v1/batch-payout?payment_type=wave_money&country_code=SN. All recipients in a single batch must use the same payment provider and country.
The request body is an array of payout objects (not a wrapper object). Do not nest the array inside a property.

This endpoint returns HTTP 202 (accepted). Processing is asynchronous. Bictorys fires a webhook event for each individual payout when it completes or fails. Do not treat 202 as confirmation that all payouts succeeded.
Use the X-Batch-Reference header and idempotency-key header on retries. The same idempotency-key returns E400-0: Same Idempotency is passed if the batch was already accepted.
Sandbox base URL is https://api.test.bictorys.com — the production URL is https://api.bictorys.com. Sandbox bulk payouts do not send real money.
